items.situation: two friends discussing a sci-fi movie one just saw — great twists but too long

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I finally watched that new space mystery film last night. Honestly, the plot twists totally caught me off guard.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Really? Everyone keeps telling me to see it. Was it worth it?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The story was clever, but it ran almost three hours. My back was killing me by the end.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Three hours? That's a lot. I get restless after ninety minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Then maybe wait for the streaming version. You can pause it whenever you want.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good call. I'll definitely do that instead of sitting in a theater that long.

questions.stem: What complaint does the woman have about the movie?

options.A: It was too long to sit through comfortably
options.B: The plot twists were too confusing
options.C: The ending felt rushed and incomplete
options.D: The streaming version had poor picture quality

questions.explanation: 女士明确说电影将近三个小时，看到最后她的背都疼了，所以正确答案是A（太长坐不住）。B错误：她说剧情转折很聪明、出乎意料，并没说混乱。C属于未提及的相关内容，她从未抱怨结尾仓促。D错误：流媒体版本是后面给的建议，她没评价其画质。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do?

options.A: Buy a ticket to see the movie in theaters
options.C: Watch the movie on a streaming service

questions.explanation: 女士建议他等流媒体版本，可以随时暂停，男士回答'Good call'并说会那样做，所以会在流媒体上看，答案是C。A是他明确拒绝的选项（不想在影院坐那么久）。B属于过度推断，他并没说完全不看。D是无关的下一步，对话中从未提到看影评。

items.situation: two friends recommending a true-crime podcast to each other

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that true-crime podcast everyone's talking about? The episodes are only twenty minutes each.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Twenty minutes? That's perfect for my walk to the lab. What's it called?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: It's called Cold Files. The host actually interviews retired detectives, so it feels really authentic.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that sounds great. I usually just listen to music, but I've been wanting something new.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Start with episode four. That one hooked me completely.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Episode four, got it. I'll put it on tomorrow morning.

questions.stem: According to the woman, why does the podcast feel authentic?

options.A: It is produced by a famous news network
options.C: Each episode is only twenty minutes long
options.D: It is based on a best-selling book

questions.explanation: 女士说主持人采访退休的警探，所以听起来很真实，答案是B。A属于未提及的相关内容，对话从未提到新闻机构制作。C错误：二十分钟说的是时长，是男士觉得方便的原因，不是'真实感'的来源。D属于未提及内容，没提到任何畅销书。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tomorrow morning?

options.A: Listen to music as he usually does
options.C: Search for a different podcast to try
options.D: Listen to episode four of the podcast

questions.explanation: 男士说'Episode four, got it. I'll put it on tomorrow morning'，明确表示明早会听第四集，答案是D。A是他正想替换掉的旧习惯（usually just listen to music），被他放弃了。B是对'采访警探'信息的过度推断，他自己并不会去采访。C是无关的下一步，他已经决定听这个播客，不会再找别的。

items.situation: two students talking about which elective class to take next term

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I can't decide between the photography elective and the public speaking one for next term. They both meet on Tuesdays.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, public speaking helped me a ton. I used to freeze up during presentations, but not anymore.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hmm, that's actually my biggest weakness. I get so nervous presenting research.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Then it's a no-brainer. The professor gives you tons of practice in a low-pressure setting.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Photography just sounded more fun, but you're right, I should pick what helps my career.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Exactly. You can always pick up photography as a hobby later.

questions.stem: What are the two students mainly discussing?

options.A: How to improve their photography skills
options.B: A scheduling conflict with their Tuesday labs
options.C: Which elective class the woman should take
options.D: A presentation they have to give together

questions.explanation: 整段对话围绕女士在摄影选修课和演讲选修课之间犹豫，男士帮她做选择，所以主旨是C（女士该选哪门选修课）。A太狭窄，摄影只是其中一个被讨论的选项，不是主题。B错误：周二只是两门课时间相同，并非与实验课冲突。D是误读情境，他们并没有要一起做展示。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next term?

options.A: Enroll in the public speaking elective
options.B: Take both elective classes at once
options.D: Switch all her classes to a different day

questions.explanation: 女士承认演讲是她最大的弱点，并说'你说得对，我应该选对职业有帮助的'，倾向于选演讲课，答案是A。B是过度推断，两门课时间冲突无法同时选。C是无关且夸张的下一步，她从未说要放弃研究项目。D是无关下一步，对话中没提到换上课日期。

items.situation: two friends planning a surprise birthday party for a classmate

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So Daniel's birthday is Friday. I think we should throw him a surprise party at the common room.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Love it. But how do we get him there without him suspecting anything?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'll tell him we're meeting to review notes for the chemistry final. He never skips a study session.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Smart. I'll handle the cake and decorations, then. Chocolate's his favorite, right?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely chocolate. And let's tell everyone to arrive by six, before he gets there at six-thirty.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Perfect. I'll text the group chat tonight so nobody spills the secret early.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly trying to do?

options.B: Decide what gift to buy a classmate
options.D: Plan a surprise party for their friend

questions.explanation: 整段对话都在策划周五给Daniel办一个惊喜生日派对，安排地点、蛋糕和到场时间，主旨是D。A是误读情境，学习会只是把他骗去的幌子，不是真正目的。B太狭窄且偏题，他们讨论的是蛋糕和布置，不是买礼物。C是误读情境，他们是在计划而非改期派对。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do tonight?

options.A: Buy a chocolate cake from the store
options.B: Message the group chat about the plan
options.C: Invite Daniel to the common room

questions.explanation: 男士说'I'll text the group chat tonight so nobody spills the secret early'，所以今晚会在群里发消息，答案是B。A是他负责的任务，但买蛋糕的时间没指明在今晚，且对话明确说今晚做的是发群消息。C错误：邀请Daniel是女士用学习会的借口去做的，不是男士今晚的事。D是无关下一步，那场学习会本就是幌子，没人会取消它。

items.situation: a student asking a shop about repairing a cracked phone screen

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hi, I dropped my phone and the screen's badly cracked. Can you fix it today?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Let me check the model. Ah, for this one we don't keep the screen part in stock, so I'd have to order it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no. How long would that take? I really rely on this phone for work.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The part usually arrives in two business days. Once it's here, the repair itself takes about an hour.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Two days without a phone is rough. Is there any way to speed it up?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I can offer you a basic loaner phone while you wait, at no extra charge. Just leave a deposit.

questions.stem: Why is the woman speaking with the man?

options.A: To have her cracked phone screen repaired
options.B: To buy a brand-new phone from the shop
options.C: To return a phone she recently purchased
options.D: To complain that a past repair failed

questions.explanation: 女士一开口就说手机摔了、屏幕碎得很厉害，问能不能今天修，全程围绕修屏，所以来意是A。B是另一个合理的店内话题，但她要的是维修而非买新机。C偏题，她没有要退货。D是误读情境，对话里没有任何过往维修失败的内容。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do?

options.A: Buy a replacement phone instead of repairing
options.B: Take her phone to a different repair shop
options.C: Accept a loaner phone while she waits
options.D: Demand that the repair be finished today

questions.explanation: 女士担心两天没手机很难熬，店员提出可以免费借给她一部备用机只需押金，这正好解决她的顾虑，所以她很可能接受借用机，答案是C。A是无关下一步，她想修而非换机。B是无对话依据的无关选项，没提去别家。D是被现实否定的过度要求——零件要两天才到，今天根本修不了。
